settings.heading: Conch Confident: Everything You Need to Know Before You Pierce (Outer Conch)
settings.subheading:
Whether its hoops or studs, this piercing is a great centre of attraction for a growing ear stack for both piercing amateurs and enthusiasts. Read more for outer conch hoops.

JEWELLERY: Segment Clickers, Captive Bead Rings (BCR's), Horseshoe Barbells (CBB's)

STANDARD SIZE: 16G (1.2mm), and occasionally 14G (1.6mm) & 8-10mm inner diameter

DOWNSIZE: 10-12mm diameter hoops for conch due to the bigger diameter of piercig. Opt for smaller hoops and downsize if needed once the piercing has fully healed for a more sleek, flush look.

HEALING TIME: 6-12 months for full healing as it is a cartilage piercing and tissue is thick and tough in this area. Initial stages of healing can be from 3-6 months.

PAIN LEVEL: Moderate-High (6-8/10) on the pain scale as the conch is a thicker piece of cartilage, more intense than the helix or lobe.

AFTERCARE: Clean your piercings 2-3 a day with Saline solution and restrain from fiddling with the piercing. Avoid sleeping on the pierced side and it is best to wait until the piercing has healed before deciding to get another one.

PRO-TIP: As conch jewellery can be the centrepiece of your ear stack, make sure its both what you love to wear as well as a perfect fit that brings together the top and bottom half of your ear.

settings.heading: Inner Conch 101
settings.subheading:
The perfectly unique inner piercing that's great for those that want simplicity with statement. Sitting in the bowl of the ear, this piercing offers endless possibilities for styling the deep shell of the ear.

JEWELLERY: Flatback Studs, Ball End Studs

STANDARD SIZE: 16G (1.2mm), and occasionally 14G (1.6mm) & 8-10mm inner diameter

DOWNSIZE: 6-10mm studs are perfect depending on the anatomy of the individual and preference. A more comfortable, neat fit.

HEALING TIME:6-12 months for full healing as it is a cartilage piercing and tissue is thick and tough in this area. Initial stages of healing can be from 3-6 months.

PAIN LEVEL: Moderate-High (6-8/10) on the pain scale as the conch is a thicker piece of cartilage, more intense than the helix or lobe.

AFTERCARE: Clean your piercings 2-3 a day with Saline solution and restrain from fiddling with the piercing. Avoid sleeping on the pierced side and it is best to wait until the piercing has healed before deciding to get another one.

PRO-TIP: As inner conch jewellery can be very different to outer conch jewellery, it's best to always see what looks great on the curvature of your ear. Each piece is different for individuals and their ear stack, opt for one that suits both your anatomy and preferred style.
